criteria: The agent has read Section 3.1 completely to understand the CLIP architecture, the classification probability formula, and prompt tuning methodology.
The agent has read Section 3.2.1 completely to understand the Sharpness-Aware Prompt Tuning (SAPT) method and its role in identifying the training flat minimum.
The agent has read Section 3.2.2 completely to understand the Sharpness-based Test Sample Selection (STSS) method and how it aligns training and test flat minima.
The agent has read Section 5.1 completely to understand the experimental setup, including datasets, backbone models, hyperparameters, and evaluation protocols.
The agent has read Table 1 to check the domain generalization results on ImageNet and its OOD variants (IN-A, IN-V2, IN-R, IN-Sketch) with CLIP-ViT-B/16.
The agent has read Table 2 to check the cross-dataset generalization results across 10 fine-grained classification datasets.
The agent has read Section 5.3 to understand the ablation study on main components (SAPT, STSS, and their combination FGA) and the hyperparameter analysis of ρ'.
The agent has read Figure 3 to understand the ablation on the key hyperparameter ρ' and its effect on test accuracy and sample retention on ImageNet-R.
The plan includes the setup of the CLIP model (ViT-B/16 and ResNet50 encoders) and integration with the CoOp prompt tuning framework.
The plan includes implementing the SAPT training objective (Equation 3) combining cross-entropy loss and sharpness penalty.
The plan includes implementing the optimal perturbation computation ε* via gradient normalization (Equation 4) for the SAPT training step.
The plan includes implementing the STSS sharpness-based scoring mechanism using R random Gaussian perturbations at test time (Equation 5).
The plan includes the batch computation of perturbed text features across R perturbations for all K categories (Equation 6).
The plan includes the selection of the top-s augmented test samples with the lowest sharpness-based scores for final prediction aggregation.
The plan includes loading the ImageNet training dataset in a 16-shot setting and setting up 64 augmented views (1 original + 63 augmented) for test-time evaluation.
The plan includes loading the four ImageNet OOD datasets (ImageNet-A, ImageNet-V2, ImageNet-R, ImageNet-Sketch) for domain generalization evaluation.
The plan includes loading the 10 fine-grained cross-dataset benchmarks (Caltech101, OxfordPets, StanfordCars, Flower102, Aircraft, SUN397, DTD, Food101, UCF101, Eurosat).
The plan includes the training hyperparameters: 4 prompt tokens, SGD optimizer, learning rate 0.002, cross-dataset (5 epochs, batch 4), domain generalization (50 epochs, batch 32).
The plan includes implementing or adapting the baseline methods (at minimum TPT, DiffTPT, C-TPT, ZERO, MTA, TDA, DPE, SAR) for comparison.
The plan includes ablation experiments: SAPT+CoOp, STSS+CoOp, and full FGA (SAPT+STSS+CoOp) on the domain generalization benchmark.
The plan includes a grid search for hyperparameter ρ over [0.05, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7] and fixing λ=1, ρ'=0.5 for all experiments.
The code for the CLIP image encoder (ViT-B/16) and text encoder loading from the pretrained CLIP model has been implemented.
The code for the CLIP classification probability (Equation 1) using cosine similarity between image and text embeddings with temperature τ has been implemented.
The code for the standard cross-entropy loss (Equation 2) for prompt training has been implemented.
The code for learnable prompt parameters p = (p1, ..., pL) integrated into the CLIP text encoder following the CoOp framework has been implemented.
The code for the SAPT loss function (Equation 3) combining cross-entropy loss and sharpness term weighted by λ has been implemented.
The code for computing the optimal perturbation ε* as the normalized gradient of the CE loss scaled by ρ (Equation 4) has been implemented.
The code for the SAPT two-step training procedure (first compute ε*, then update prompt parameters using the perturbed gradient) has been implemented.
The code for generating R random Gaussian perturbation directions {ε_r / ||ε_r||} for the STSS sharpness computation at test time has been implemented.
The code for computing the STSS sharpness-based score (Equation 5) as the maximum variation in surrogate loss over R random perturbations plus the baseline loss has been implemented.
The code for batched text feature computation for all R perturbed prompts and all K categories via the text encoder (Equation 6) has been implemented.
The code for computing the perturbed surrogate loss (entropy) for each perturbation r as Equation 7 using the text features of the r-th perturbation has been implemented.

The code for computing the per-perturbation classification probability P_r(y_k|x) using cosine similarity between perturbed text features and the fixed image embedding (Equation 8) has been implemented.

The code for the full STSS inference pipeline: generate 64 augmented views, compute sharpness-based score for each, select top-s samples with lowest scores, aggregate their predictions for final output has been implemented.
The code uses ρ'=0.5 as the perturbation magnitude for test-time sharpness computation and λ=1 to balance loss and sharpness terms in both SAPT and STSS.
The code for loading ImageNet training data in the 16-shot setting (16 samples per class) for prompt tuning has been implemented.
The code for loading the four ImageNet OOD evaluation datasets (ImageNet-A, ImageNet-V2, ImageNet-R, ImageNet-Sketch) has been implemented.
The code for loading the 10 cross-dataset benchmarks (Caltech101, OxfordPets, StanfordCars, Flower102, Aircraft, SUN397, DTD, Food101, UCF101, Eurosat) has been implemented.
The code for the TPT-style data augmentation strategy producing 64 augmented views per test image has been implemented.
The code for the prompt training loop with SGD optimizer (lr=0.002), 4 prompt tokens, 50 epochs (batch 32) for domain generalization and 5 epochs (batch 4) for cross-dataset has been implemented.
The code for the STSS+CoOp ablation variant (applying STSS at test time with standard CoOp-trained prompts, without SAPT) has been implemented.
The code for the SAPT+CoOp ablation variant (training with SAPT but using standard entropy-based selection at test time, without STSS) has been implemented.
The code for the ρ' hyperparameter sensitivity analysis, varying ρ' across multiple values and sample retention sizes s on ImageNet-R, has been implemented.
The code for the CLIP-ResNet50 backbone variant of FGA (deferred to appendix) has been implemented.
The code for the full FGA framework (SAPT training + STSS test-time selection) has been executed successfully without errors on the domain generalization benchmark.
The code for the full FGA framework has been executed successfully without errors on all 10 cross-dataset generalization benchmarks.
The ablation experiments (CoOp baseline, SAPT+CoOp, STSS+CoOp, full FGA) have been executed successfully without errors on the domain generalization benchmark.
The ρ' sensitivity analysis experiment has been executed successfully without errors on ImageNet-R with varying s and ρ' values.
The baseline methods (at minimum TPT+CoOp, DiffTPT+CoOp, ZERO+CoOp, SAR+CoOp, MTA+CoOp) have been executed successfully without errors on the domain generalization benchmark.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: FGA achieves the highest overall average (68.04%) and OOD average (66.55%) on CLIP-ViT-B/16 across ImageNet, IN-A, IN-V2, IN-R, and IN-Sketch.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: FGA outperforms TPT+CoOp by an average of 4.88% on the four OOD variants (61.67% → 66.55%).
The execution outcomes match Table 1: STSS+CoOp achieves an OOD average of 64.60% and an overall average of 66.48%, outperforming all previous TTA+CoOp methods.
The execution outcomes match Table 1 ablation: SAPT+CoOp achieves a 0.92% improvement over CoOp alone (61.72% → 62.64%) on the domain generalization benchmark.
The execution outcomes match Table 1 ablation: full FGA achieves a 5.40% gain over SAPT+CoOp alone (62.64% → 68.04%), exceeding the standalone STSS improvement of 4.76%.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: FGA achieves the highest average accuracy of 67.60% across all 10 cross-dataset benchmarks.
The execution outcomes match Table 2: FGA achieves an average accuracy improvement of 1.94% over TPT+CoOp (65.66% → 67.60%) on cross-dataset generalization.
The execution outcomes match Table 2: FGA attains top-tier results on at least 6 out of 10 cross-dataset benchmarks, including notably strong performance on Caltech101 (96.96%).

The execution outcomes match Figure 3: test accuracy shows non-monotonic dependence on ρ', initially rising then falling for extreme values, with ρ'=0 (pure entropy) performing worse than non-zero ρ' values.
The execution outcomes match Figure 3: sample retention follows a similar non-monotonic trend, with accuracy peaking then declining as more augmented samples are retained.

The execution outcomes confirm the computational efficiency of FGA: inference requires approximately 0.07s per image (≈8.86× faster than TPT at 0.62s and ≈23.86× faster than DiffTPT at 1.67s) on a single GPU.
The execution outcomes confirm FGA's memory efficiency: peak GPU memory consumption is approximately 4.14 GB, which is approximately 4.67× lower than TPT (19.33 GB).
